sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The yellow container still didn't change! I've tried clicking at (31, 11), (31, 10), and (31, 9) - all columns of the yellow container - and none of them changed the container. But clicking at (10, 29) on the light green container DID change it.
What's different between the two containers?
1. Light green container: has a G (dark gray) bar to its LEFT (Node 1 at col 27)
2. Yellow container: has a G (dark gray) bar ABOVE it (Node 15 at row 27)
Maybe the G bar is the "handle" that needs to be clicked to enable cutting? For the light green container, the G bar is to the left (horizontal orientation), and for the yellow container, the G bar is above (vertical orientation).
Wait, but I already tried clicking on the G bar at (10, 27) and it didn't do anything. Let me reconsider...
Actually, looking at the light green container more carefully:
- The G bar (Node 1) is at col 27, rows 9-11 (3 pixels, vertical bar)
- The light green rectangle is at cols 28-32, rows 9-11
- When I clicked at (10, 29), the light green rectangle was cut
For the yellow container:
- The G bar (Node 15) is at row 27, cols 9-11 (3 pixels, horizontal bar)
- The yellow rectangle is at cols 9-11, rows 28-35
Maybe the cutting direction depends on the orientation of the G bar:
- Light green: G bar is vertical (on the left), so cutting is horizontal (left-right)
- Yellow: G bar is horizontal (on top), so cutting is vertical (top-bottom)
If the yellow container needs vertical cutting, I should click at a row position. But I've been clicking at row 31, which is in the middle of the container. Maybe I need to click at a specific row?
Let me try clicking at different rows to see if vertical cutting works. The yellow container spans rows 28-35. Let me try clicking at row 31, col 10 (middle column, which I already tried and it didn't work).
Wait, but I did try (31, 10) and it didn't change the container. Let me try a different row, like row 30 or row 32.
Actually, let me reconsider the whole mechanic. Maybe the cutting only works when clicking on the "cut side" of the container. For the light green container:
- The G bar is on the left
- I clicked at col 29, which is closer to the G bar side
- The cut removed everything to the right of the click
For the yellow container:
- The G bar is on top
- Maybe I need to click closer to the G bar side (top)?
- Let me try clicking at row 28 or 29 (closer to the top)
Let me try (29, 10).

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The yellow container has a G bar on TOP, suggesting vertical cutting.
# Let me try clicking closer to the top of the yellow container
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 29, 'col': 10}])
print(f"Result: board_changed={result.get('board_changed')}")

# Check the yellow container after click
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\n=== Yellow container after click ===")
for r in range(26, 37):
    print(f"  row {r}: {ascii_lines[r][7:14]}")

# Check node 16
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] == 16:
        print(f"\nNode 16: px={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['id'] == 17:
        print(f"Node 17 (red dot): boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
